title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Go Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for redirect semantics, method rewriting, credential scope, and loop control, producing auditable redirect policy engine. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http auditable redirect policy engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Resolve Location references against the current request URL and validate the resulting destination each hop. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Bound redirect hops, detect loops, retain the chain, and expose the policy decision to callers. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Manual Redirect Defense
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Go vendor-release candidate sends POST G-6 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step. Competency probe: Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Manual Redirect Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, ssrf, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Manual Redirect Defense, keep Manual Redirect Defense connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go vendor-release candidate sends POST G-6 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Competency probe: Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Manual Redirect Defense, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Following redirects is transport plumbing with no application or security semantics. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.
